title: cupania poiretii
intro: Cupania poiretii, also known as the Poison Tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae, native to China and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Cupania poiretii include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antidote for snake bites. Cupania poiretii can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used in traditional Chinese medicine.
